trackingsummarydata.subject: This week in A&S: Babies, fireworks and a new major
trackingsummarydata.title: FSSG - 09/29/25

urls.page_title: Safety first: Babies heighten adults’ perception of threats | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: In a threatening situation, the world looks more dangerous when caring for an infant, finds new research that used a virtual baby to explore parenting dynamics.
urls.page_title: Brooks and A&S partner on new option for public policy major
urls.page_summary: Students in the major will be admitted into the College of Arts & Sciences and take courses in both Brooks and A&S.
urls.page_title: Politics gets personal for China scholar
urls.page_summary: Historian Peidong Sun began her new book “Unfiltered Regard for China: French Perspectives from Mao to Xi” amid profound personal upheaval: An exit ban from China and a move to France.
urls.page_title: Revitalizing introductory physics labs with NSF support
urls.page_summary: Through The Introductory Physics Lab Institute, professor Natasha Holmes shares best practices in physics labs.
Homecoming is all about the Cornell family. Gather in Ithaca to connect, learn, relive favorite traditions, and make new memories.
urls.page_title: Music Oct. 2-5: Homecoming concerts and historical keyboards
urls.page_summary: Cornell orchestras, vocal ensembles and the Center for Historical Keyboards plan performances.
urls.page_title: 2025 Freund Prize for Creative Writing Alumni Reading - Cornell
urls.page_summary:
The Department of Literatures in English celebrates the winners of the 2025 Philip Freund Prize for Creative Writing in recognition of excellence in publication.
The alumni prize is supported by the Philip Freund '29 endowment and comes with a $5...
